author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
śrī⋅rūpa⋅vadane, śrī⋅śacīkumāra
svanāma⋅mahimā karala pracāra

jo nāma so hari, kichu nāhi bheda
so nāma satyamiti gāyati veda

sabu upaniṣad, ratnamālā⋅dyuti,
jhakamaki’ caraṇa⋅samīpe
maṅgala⋅ārati, karaba anukṣaṇa,
dviguṇita⋅pañcapradīpe

cauddabhuvana māha, deva⋅nara⋅dānava,
bhāga jākara balabān
nāmarasa⋅pīyuṣa, piyoi anukṣaṇa,
choḍata karama⋅geyāna

nityamukta punaḥ, nāma⋅upāsanā,
satata karoi sāmagāne
goloke baiṭhata, gāoye nirantara,
nāma⋅viraha nāhi jāne

sabu rasa⋅ākara, ‘hari’ itidvyakṣara,
sabubhāve karala āśraya
nāmacaraṇe pa’ḍe, bhakativinoda kahe,
tuyā pade māgahu nilaya
en_translation:
Through the mouth of Śrī Rūpa, the son of Śacī proclaimed the glories of His own Holy Name.

That Name is Hari; there is no difference. The Vedas sing that the Name is the Truth.

All the Upaniṣads, like the radiance of a garland of jewels, sparkle at His lotus feet. I will constantly perform auspicious worship with twice five lamps.

In the fourteen worlds, among demigods, men, and demons, those whose fortune is strong constantly drink the nectar of the Holy Name, giving up fruitive activities and speculative knowledge.

Eternally liberated souls worship the Holy Name, always singing in Vedic melodies. Seated in Goloka, they sing ceaselessly; they know no separation from the Holy Name.

The two syllables 'Hari', the source of all mellows, are the shelter in all circumstances. Falling at the feet of the Holy Name, Bhaktivinoda says: "At Your feet, I beg for a dwelling."
